430:             bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
431:             {
432:                 auto current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
433:                 auto newVer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
434: 
435:                 // Compare version components
436:                 size_t minSize = std::min(current.size(), newVer.size());
437: 
438:                 for (size_t i = 0; i < minSize; ++i)
439:                 {
440:                     if (newVer[i] > current[i])
441:                     {
442:                         return true;
443:                     }
444:                     else if (newVer[i] < current[i])
445:                     {
446:                         return false;
447:                     }
448:                     // If equal, continue to next component
449:                 }
450: 
451:                 // If all compared components are equal, the longer version is considered newer
452:                 // For example: 1.2.3 vs 1.2.3.1 -> 1.2.3.1 is newer
453:                 return newVer.size() > current.size();
454:             }
455:             std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
456:             {
457:                 if (!has_serial_number_)
458:                 {
459:                     return "{}";
460:                 }
461: 
462:                 std::string hmac_hex;
463: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
464:                 uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
465: 
466:                 // 使用Key0计算HMAC
467:                 esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
468:                 if (ret != ESP_OK)
469:                 {
470:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
471:                     return "{}";
472:                 }
473: 
474:                 for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
475:                 {
476:                     char buffer[3];
477:                     sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
478:                     hmac_hex += buffer;
479:                 }
480: #endif
481: 
482:                 cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
483:                 cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
484:                 cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
485:                 cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
486:                 cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
487:                 auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
488:                 std::string json(json_str);
489:                 cJSON_free(json_str);
490:                 cJSON_Delete(payload);
491: 
492:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
493:                 return json;
494:             }
495: 
496:             esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
497:             {
498:                 if (!has_activation_challenge_)
499:                 {
500:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
501:                     return ESP_FAIL;
502:                 }
503: 
504:                 std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
505:                 if (url.back() != '/')
506:                 {
507:                     url += "/activate";
508:                 }
509:                 else
510:                 {
511:                     url += "activate";
512:                 }
513: 
514:                 auto http = SetupHttp();
515: 
516:                 std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
517:                 http->SetContent(std::move(data));
518: 
519:                 if (!http->Open("POST", url))
520:                 {
521:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
522:                     return ESP_FAIL;
523:                 }
524: 
525:                 auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
526:                 if (status_code == 202)
527:                 {
528:                     return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
529:                 }
530:                 if (status_code != 200)
531:                 {
532:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
533:                     return ESP_FAIL;
534:                 }
535: 
536:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
537:                 return ESP_OK;
538:             }

296: void Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid()
297: {
298:     auto partition = esp_ota_get_running_partition();
299:     if (strcmp(partition->label, "factory") == 0)
300:     {
301:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running from factory partition, skipping");
302:         return;
303:     }
304: 
305:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running partition: %s", partition->label);
306:     esp_ota_img_states_t state;
307:     if (esp_ota_get_state_partition(partition, &state) != ESP_OK)
308:     {
309:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get state of partition");
310:         return;
311:     }
312: 
313:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)
314:     {
315:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
316:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();
317:     }
318: }
319: 
320: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
321: {
322:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
323:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
324:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
325:     if (update_partition == NULL)
326:     {
327:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
328:         return false;
329:     }
330: 
331:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
332:     bool image_header_checked = false;
333:     std::string image_header;
334: 
335:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
336:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
337:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
338:     {
339:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
340:         return false;
341:     }
342: 
343:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
344:     {
345:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
346:         return false;
347:     }
348: 
349:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
350:     if (content_length == 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {
390:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
391:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
392:             {
393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

445: bool Ota::StartUpgrade(std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
446: {
447:     return Upgrade(firmware_url_, callback);
448: }

450: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
451: {
452:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
453:     std::stringstream ss(version);
454:     std::string segment;
455: 
456:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
457:     {
458:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
459:     }
460: 
461:     return versionNumbers;
462: }
463: 
464: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
465: {
466:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
467:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
468: 
469:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
470:     {
471:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
472:         {
473:             return true;
474:         }
475:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
476:         {
477:             return false;
478:         }
479:     }
480: 
481:     return newer.size() > current.size();
482: }

484: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
485: {
486:     if (!has_serial_number_)
487:     {
488:         return "{}";
489:     }
490: 
491:     std::string hmac_hex;
492: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
493:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
494: 
495:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
496:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
497:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
498:     {
499:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
500:         return "{}";
501:     }
502: 
503:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
504:     {
505:         char buffer[3];
506:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
507:         hmac_hex += buffer;
508:     }
509: #endif
510: 
511:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
512:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
513:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
514:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
515:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
516:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
517:     std::string json(json_str);
518:     cJSON_free(json_str);
519:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
520: 
521:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
522:     return json;
523: }

526: {
527: }

56: esp_err_t SystemInfo::PrintTaskCpuUsage(TickType_t xTicksToWait)
57: {
58: #define ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET 5
59:     TaskStatus_t *start_array = NULL, *end_array = NULL;
60:     UBaseType_t start_array_size, end_array_size;
61:     configRUN_TIME_COUNTER_TYPE start_run_time, end_run_time;
62:     esp_err_t ret;
63:     uint32_t total_elapsed_time;
64: 
65:     // Allocate array to store current task states
66:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
67:     start_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * start_array_size);
68:     if (start_array == NULL)
69:     {
70:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
71:         goto exit;
72:     }
73:     // Get current task states
74:     start_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(start_array, start_array_size, &start_run_time);
75:     if (start_array_size == 0)
76:     {
77:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
78:         goto exit;
79:     }
80: 
81:     vTaskDelay(xTicksToWait);
82: 
83:     // Allocate array to store tasks states post delay
84:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks() + ARRAY_SIZE_OFFSET;
85:     end_array = (TaskStatus_t *)malloc(sizeof(TaskStatus_t) * end_array_size);
86:     if (end_array == NULL)
87:     {
88:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
89:         goto exit;
90:     }
91:     // Get post delay task states
92:     end_array_size = uxTaskGetSystemState(end_array, end_array_size, &end_run_time);
93:     if (end_array_size == 0)
94:     {
95:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
96:         goto exit;
97:     }
98: 
99:     // Calculate total_elapsed_time in units of run time stats clock period.
100:     total_elapsed_time = (end_run_time - start_run_time);
101:     if (total_elapsed_time == 0)
102:     {
103:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
104:         goto exit;
105:     }
106: 
107:     printf("| Task | Run Time | Percentage\n");
108:     // Match each task in start_array to those in the end_array
109:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
110:     {
111:         int k = -1;
112:         for (int j = 0; j < end_array_size; j++)
113:         {
114:             if (start_array[i].xHandle == end_array[j].xHandle)
115:             {
116:                 k = j;
117:                 // Mark that task have been matched by overwriting their handles
118:                 start_array[i].xHandle = NULL;
119:                 end_array[j].xHandle = NULL;
120:                 break;
121:             }
122:         }
123:         // Check if matching task found
124:         if (k >= 0)
125:         {
126:             uint32_t task_elapsed_time = end_array[k].ulRunTimeCounter - start_array[i].ulRunTimeCounter;
127:             uint32_t percentage_time = (task_elapsed_time * 100UL) / (total_elapsed_time * CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES);
128:             printf("| %-16s | %8lu | %4lu%%\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName, task_elapsed_time, percentage_time);
129:         }
130:     }
131: 
132:     // Print unmatched tasks
133:     for (int i = 0; i < start_array_size; i++)
134:     {
135:         if (start_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
136:         {
137:             printf("| %s | Deleted\n", start_array[i].pcTaskName);
138:         }
139:     }
140:     for (int i = 0; i < end_array_size; i++)
141:     {
142:         if (end_array[i].xHandle != NULL)
143:         {
144:             printf("| %s | Created\n", end_array[i].pcTaskName);
145:         }
146:     }
147:     ret = ESP_OK;
148: 
149: exit: // Common return path
150:     free(start_array);
151:     free(end_array);
152:     return ret;
153: }

73:     if (start_array == NULL)
74:     {
75:         ret = ESP_ERR_NO_MEM;
76:         goto exit;
77:     }

80:     if (start_array_size == 0)

82:         ret = ESP_ERR_INVALID_SIZE;
83:         goto exit;
84:     }
85:

88:     // Allocate array to store tasks states post delay

101:         goto exit;
102:     }
103:

138: void Es8388AudioCodec::CreateDuplexChannels(gpio_num_t mclk, gpio_num_t bclk, gpio_num_t ws, gpio_num_t dout, gpio_num_t din)
139: {
140:     assert(input_sample_rate_ == output_sample_rate_);
141: 
142:     i2s_chan_config_t chan_cfg = {
143:         .id = I2S_NUM_0,
144:         .role = I2S_ROLE_MASTER,
145:         .dma_desc_num = AUDIO_CODEC_DMA_DESC_NUM,
146:         .dma_frame_num = AUDIO_CODEC_DMA_FRAME_NUM,
147:         .auto_clear_after_cb = true,
148:         .auto_clear_before_cb = false,
149:         .intr_priority = 0,
150:     };
151:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2s_new_channel(&chan_cfg, &tx_handle_, &rx_handle_));
152: 
153:     i2s_std_config_t std_cfg = {
154:         .clk_cfg = {
155:             .sample_rate_hz = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
156:             .clk_src = I2S_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
157:             .ext_clk_freq_hz = 0,
158:             .mclk_multiple = I2S_MCLK_MULTIPLE_256},
159:         .slot_cfg = {.data_bit_width = I2S_DATA_BIT_WIDTH_16BIT, .slot_bit_width = I2S_SLOT_BIT_WIDTH_AUTO, .slot_mode = I2S_SLOT_MODE_STEREO, .slot_mask = I2S_STD_SLOT_BOTH, .ws_width = I2S_DATA_BIT_WIDTH_16BIT, .ws_pol = false, .bit_shift = true, .left_align = true, .big_endian = false, .bit_order_lsb = false},
160:         .gpio_cfg = {.mclk = mclk, .bclk = bclk, .ws = ws, .dout = dout, .din = din, .invert_flags = {.mclk_inv = false, .bclk_inv = false, .ws_inv = false}}};
161: 
162:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2s_channel_init_std_mode(tx_handle_, &std_cfg));
163:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2s_channel_init_std_mode(rx_handle_, &std_cfg));
164:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Duplex channels created");
165: }
166: 
167: void Es8388AudioCodec::SetOutputVolume(int volume)
168: {
169:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, volume));
170:     AudioCodec::SetOutputVolume(volume);
171: }
172: 
173: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableInput(bool enable)
174: {
175:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
176:     if (enable == input_enabled_)
177:     {
178:         return;
179:     }
180:     if (enable)
181:     {
182:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
183:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
184:             .channel = (uint8_t)input_channels_,
185:             .channel_mask = ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(0),
186:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)input_sample_rate_,
187:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
188:         };
189:         if (input_reference_)
190:         {
191:             fs.channel_mask |= ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(1);
192:         }
193:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(input_dev_, &fs));
194:         if (input_reference_)
195:         {
196:             uint8_t gain = (11 << 4) + 0;
197:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, 0x09, 1, &gain, 1);
198:         }
199:         else
200:         {
201:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain(input_dev_, input_gain_));
202:         }
203:     }
204:     else
205:     {
206:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
207:     }
208:     AudioCodec::EnableInput(enable);
209: }
210: 
211: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableOutput(bool enable)
212: {
213:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(data_if_mutex_);
214:     if (enable == output_enabled_)
215:     {
216:         return;
217:     }
218:     if (enable)
219:     {
220:         esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t fs = {
221:             .bits_per_sample = 16,
222:             .channel = 1,
223:             .channel_mask = 0,
224:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)output_sample_rate_,
225:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
226:         };
227:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_open(output_dev_, &fs));
228:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));
229: 
230:         // Set analog output volume to 0dB, default is -45dB
231:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB
232:         if (input_reference_)
233:         {
234:             reg_val = 27;
235:         }
236:         uint8_t regs[] = {46, 47, 48, 49}; // HP_LVOL, HP_RVOL, SPK_LVOL, SPK_RVOL
237:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)
238:         {
239:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, reg, 1, &reg_val, 1);
240:         }
241: 
242:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
243:         {
244:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(output_dev_));
250:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
251:         {
252:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
253:         }
254:     }
255:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);
256: }
257: 
258: int Es8388AudioCodec::Read(int16_t *dest, int samples)
259: {
260:     if (input_enabled_)
261:     {
262:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_read(input_dev_, (void *)dest, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
263:     }
264:     return samples;
265: }
266: 
267: int Es8388AudioCodec::Write(const int16_t *data, int samples)
268: {
269:     if (output_enabled_ && output_dev_ && data != nullptr)
270:     {
271:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK_WITHOUT_ABORT(esp_codec_dev_write(output_dev_, (void *)data, samples * sizeof(int16_t)));
272:     }
273:     return samples;
274: }
275: 
</file>

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code introduces additional functionality beyond what's required by the task query. While it correctly implements the duplex channel creation using I2S APIs and maintains the same core logic for channel configuration and initialization, it also includes unnecessary code for creating input and output codec devices via esp_codec_dev_new(), which are not part of the original function signature or requirements.

Semantic Correctness (75/100):
- The core I2S channel setup and configuration matches the ground truth
- The assertion and logging statements are preserved
- However, the addition of codec device creation changes the function's purpose and scope

Structure Similarity (85/100):
- Function signature exactly matches
- Control flow and API call sequence are very similar
- Variable names and struct initialization patterns match closely
- Only minor formatting differences exist

Functional Completeness (60/100):
- The primary goal of creating duplex I2S channels is achieved
- But extra functionality unrelated to the specified task is included
- The added codec device creation makes this more than just a duplex channel creator
- Missing some minor details like exact enum values in comments that could improve readability

However, there are several critical differences that affect semantic correctness and completeness.

Structure Similarity (85/100): The overall structure is very similar with matching function signature, mutex usage, conditional blocks, and API calls. The formatting and control flow are nearly identical, with only minor differences in brace placement and comment style.

Functional Completeness (60/100): The generated code lacks several key features from the ground truth. It omits the input_reference_ check and related channel mask logic, removes the special gain setting for reference channels, and fails to handle the case where input_reference_ is true. These omissions make the generated code functionally incomplete for the full use case.

122: void Es8388AudioCodec::EnableInput(bool enable)
123: {

125:     if (enable == input_enabled_)
126:     {

128:     }

134:             .channel_mask = ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK(0),
135:             .sample_rate = (uint32_t)input_sample_rate_,
136:             .mclk_multiple = 0,
137:         };

141:         }

143:         if (input_reference_)
144:         {
145:             uint8_t gain = (11 << 4) + 0;
146:             ctrl_if_->write_reg(ctrl_if_, 0x09, 1, &gain, 1);
147:         }
148:         else
149:         {
150:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain(input_dev_, input_gain_));
151:         }
152:     }
153:     else
154:     {
155:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_close(input_dev_));
156:     }
157:     AudioCodec::EnableInput(enable);
158: }

166:     }

175:         };

177:         ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol(output_dev_, output_volume_));

180:         uint8_t reg_val = 30; // 0dB

183:             reg_val = 27;

186:         for (uint8_t reg : regs)

189:         }
190:

192:         {
193:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 1);
194:         }
195:     }

199:         if (pa_pin_ != GPIO_NUM_NC)
200:         {
201:             gpio_set_level(pa_pin_, 0);
202:         }
203:     }
204:     AudioCodec::EnableOutput(enable);

178:     // keep about 2 seconds of data, detect duration is 30ms (sample_rate == 16000, chunksize == 512)
179:     while (wake_word_pcm_.size() > 2000 / 30)
180:     {

182:     }
183: }

185: void AfeWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData()
186: {
187:     const size_t stack_size = 4096 * 7;
188:     wake_word_opus_.clear();
189:     if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
190:     {
191:         wake_word_encode_task_stack_ = (StackType_t *)heap_caps_malloc(stack_size, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM);
192:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_stack_ != nullptr);
193:     }
194:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
195:     {
196:         wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = (StaticTask_t *)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(StaticTask_t), MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
197:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ != nullptr);
198:     }
199: 
200:     wake_word_encode_task_ = xTaskCreateStatic([](void *arg)
201:                                                {
202:         auto this_ = (AfeWakeWord*)arg;
203:         {
204:             auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
205:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
206:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
207: 
208:             int packets = 0;
209:             for (auto& pcm: this_->wake_word_pcm_) {
210:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
211:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
212:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
213:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
214:                 });
215:                 packets++;
216:             }
217:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
218: 
219:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
220:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
221: 
222:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
223:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
224:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
225:         }
226:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
227: }
228: 
229: bool AfeWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
230: {
231:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
232:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
233:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
234:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
235:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
236:     return !opus.empty();
237: }
238:

187: {

206:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
207:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
208: 
209:             int packets = 0;

211:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
212:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
213:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
214:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
215:                 });
216:                 packets++;
217:             }
218:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
219: 
220:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
221:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
222: 
223:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
224:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
225:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
226:         }
227:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
228: }

230: bool AfeWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
231: {

233:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
234:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
235:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
236:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
237:     return !opus.empty();

146: void AfeWakeWord::AudioDetectionTask()
147: {
148:     auto fetch_size = afe_iface_->get_fetch_chunksize(afe_data_);
149:     auto feed_size = afe_iface_->get_feed_chunksize(afe_data_);
150:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Audio detection task started, feed size: %d fetch size: %d",
151:              feed_size, fetch_size);
152: 
153:     while (true)
154:     {
155:         xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, DETECTION_RUNNING_EVENT, pdFALSE, pdTRUE, portMAX_DELAY);
156: 
157:         auto res = afe_iface_->fetch_with_delay(afe_data_, portMAX_DELAY);
158:         if (res == nullptr || res->ret_value == ESP_FAIL)
159:         {
160:             continue;
161:             ;
162:         }
163: 
164:         // Store the wake word data for voice recognition, like who is speaking
165:         StoreWakeWordData(res->data, res->data_size / sizeof(int16_t));
166: 
167:         if (res->wakeup_state == WAKENET_DETECTED)
168:         {
169:             Stop();
170:             last_detected_wake_word_ = wake_words_[res->wakenet_model_index - 1];
171: 
172:             if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
173:             {
174:                 wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
175:             }
176:         }
177:     }

216:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
217:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);

225:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
226:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
227: 
228:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
229:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
230:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
231:         }

153:     {
154:         return;
155:     }
156: 
157:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
158:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
159:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
160:     {
161:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
162:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
163:         {
164:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
165:         }
166: 
167:         StoreWakeWordData(mono_data);
168:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));
169:     }
170:     else
171:     {
172:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
173:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
174:     }
175: 
176:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
177:     {
178:         return;
179:     }
180:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
181:     {
182:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
183:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
184:         {
185:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
186:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);

196:                 }
197:             }

200:     }
201:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
202:     {
203:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
204:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
205:     }

116: void CustomWakeWord::Feed(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
117: {
118:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr || !running_)
119:     {
120:         return;
121:     }
122: 
123:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
124:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
125:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
126:     {
127:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
128:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
129:         {
130:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
131:         }
132: 
133:         StoreWakeWordData(mono_data);
134:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));
135:     }
136:     else
137:     {
138:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
139:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
140:     }
141: 
142:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
143:     {
144:         return;
145:     }
146:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
147:     {
148:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
149:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
150:         {
151:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
152:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
153:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
154:             if (command.action == "wake")
155:             {
156:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
157:                 running_ = false;
158: 
159:                 if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
160:                 {
161:                     wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
162:                 }
163:             }
164:         }
165:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
166:     }
167:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
168:     {
169:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
170:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
171:     }
172: }

194: void CustomWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData()
195: {
196:     const size_t stack_size = 4096 * 7;
197:     wake_word_opus_.clear();
198:     if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         wake_word_encode_task_stack_ = (StackType_t *)heap_caps_malloc(stack_size, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM);
201:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_stack_ != nullptr);
202:     }
203:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
204:     {
205:         wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = (StaticTask_t *)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(StaticTask_t), MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
206:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ != nullptr);
207:     }
208: 
209:     wake_word_encode_task_ = xTaskCreateStatic([](void *arg)
210:                                                {
211:         auto this_ = (CustomWakeWord*)arg;
212:         {
213:             auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
214:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
215:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
216: 
217:             int packets = 0;
218:             for (auto& pcm: this_->wake_word_pcm_) {
219:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
220:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
221:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
222:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
223:                 });
224:                 packets++;
225:             }
226:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
227: 
228:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
229:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
230: 
231:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
232:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
233:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
234:         }
235:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
236: }
237: 
238: bool CustomWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
239: {
240:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
241:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
242:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
243:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
244:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
245:     return !opus.empty();

The generated code attempts to implement the Initialize function but has several issues that affect its correctness and completeness:

1. **Semantic Correctness (75/100)**: The overall logic is mostly correct, but there are critical differences. The ground truth handles a special case when models_list is null by initializing a default model and setting up custom wake word configurations. The generated code doesn't handle this case at all and instead assumes models is always valid. Additionally, the generated code uses GetSystemLanguage() which isn't present in the ground truth, and it lacks the threshold configuration for custom wake words.

2. **Structure Similarity (60/100)**: The structure is somewhat similar but has significant differences. The ground truth has a clear conditional branch for handling null models_list, while the generated code only checks for null pointers at the beginning. Control flow differs notably in how models are handled and where ParseWakenetModelConfig() is called. The function signature and basic structure are similar, but internal organization varies.

3. **Functional Completeness (65/100)**: The generated code misses several key functionalities from the ground truth:
   - No handling of the null models_list case
   - Missing custom wake word configuration (threshold, command list population)
   - Missing initialization of commands_ with custom wake word data
   - Different approach to language handling (using GetSystemLanguage vs hardcoding "cn")
   - Missing the check for models_ being null or having num == -1
   - Missing explicit language assignment when models_list is null

Overall, while the generated code shows some understanding of the task, it fails to capture the full complexity and specific requirements of the original implementation.

173:     {
174:         return;
175:     }
176: 
177:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
178:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
179:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
180:     {
181:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
182:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
183:         {
184:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
185:         }

188:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));

192:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
193:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
194:     }
195: 
196:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
197:     {
198:         return;
199:     }
200:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
201:     {
202:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
203:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
204:         {
205:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
206:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
207:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
208:             if (command.action == "wake")
209:             {
210:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
211:                 running_ = false;
212: 
213:                 if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
214:                 {
215:                     wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
216:                 }
217:             }
218:         }
219:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
220:     }
221:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
222:     {
223:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
224:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
225:     }

It also doesn't use any of the actual model API calls or data access patterns. The function loses its dynamic behavior and becomes a static implementation that cannot adapt to different model configurations or handle error conditions properly.

173:     {

195:

197:     {
198:         return;
199:     }
200:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
201:     {
202:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
203:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
204:         {

206:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
207:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
208:             if (command.action == "wake")
209:             {
210:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
211:                 running_ = false;
212:

217:             }

220:     }
221:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)

241: void CustomWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData()
242: {
243:     const size_t stack_size = 4096 * 7;
244:     wake_word_opus_.clear();
245:     if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
246:     {
247:         wake_word_encode_task_stack_ = (StackType_t *)heap_caps_malloc(stack_size, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM);
248:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_stack_ != nullptr);
249:     }
250:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
251:     {
252:         wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = (StaticTask_t *)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(StaticTask_t), MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
253:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ != nullptr);
254:     }
255: 
256:     wake_word_encode_task_ = xTaskCreateStatic([](void *arg)
257:                                                {
258:         auto this_ = (CustomWakeWord*)arg;
259:         {
260:             auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
261:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
262:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
263: 
264:             int packets = 0;
265:             for (auto& pcm: this_->wake_word_pcm_) {
266:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
267:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
268:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
269:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
270:                 });
271:                 packets++;
272:             }
273:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
274: 
275:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
276:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
277: 
278:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
279:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
280:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
281:         }
282:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
283: }
284: 
285: bool CustomWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
286: {
287:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
288:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
289:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
290:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
291:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
292:     return !opus.empty();
293: }

</file>

qualitative.Functional Completeness (40/100): The generated code is missing the core functionality of limiting stored data to ~2 seconds. The code is incomplete in terms of the actual requirement to maintain bounded memory usage.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (65/100):
The generated code attempts to replicate the core functionality but has significant semantic issues. While both aim to encode wake word PCM data to OPUS format, the generated version uses dynamic task creation with xTaskCreate instead of xTaskCreateStatic, and lacks the proper static task allocation pattern. It also incorrectly processes PCM data by combining all chunks into one buffer rather than processing them individually as in the ground truth. The encoding logic is incomplete and simulated rather than implementing actual Opus encoding.

Structure Similarity (55/100):
There are notable structural differences. The ground truth uses xTaskCreateStatic with pre-allocated static buffers and a lambda function, while the generated code uses xTaskCreate with dynamic allocation and a separate function pointer. The variable names differ (CONFIG_ macros vs direct constants), and the control flow structure is different. The generated code doesn't follow the same initialization pattern for stack and buffer allocation.

Functional Completeness (40/100):
The generated code misses several key functionalities from the ground truth. It doesn't implement the actual Opus encoding process, lacks proper error handling for the encoding task, doesn't handle the encoding loop over individual PCM chunks, and doesn't properly manage the completion signaling via empty packet insertion. It also removes the encoder complexity setting and doesn't include the timing measurement functionality. The generated code appears to be a partial implementation that fails to capture the full scope of the original function's responsibilities.

88:                     cJSON *action = cJSON_GetObjectItem(command, "action");
89:                     if (cJSON_IsString(command_name) && cJSON_IsString(text) && cJSON_IsString(action))
90:                     {
91:                         commands_.push_back({command_name->valuestring, text->valuestring, action->valuestring});
92:                         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Command: %s, Text: %s, Action: %s", command_name->valuestring, text->valuestring, action->valuestring);
93:                     }
94:                 }
95:             }
96:         }
97:     }
98:     cJSON_Delete(root);
99: }
100: 
101: bool CustomWakeWord::Initialize(AudioCodec *codec, srmodel_list_t *models_list)
102: {
103:     codec_ = codec;
104:     commands_.clear();
105: 
106:     if (models_list == nullptr)
107:     {
108:         language_ = "cn";
109:         models_ = esp_srmodel_init("model");
110: #ifdef CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
111:         threshold_ = CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD_THRESHOLD / 100.0f;
112:         commands_.push_back({CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD, CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD_DISPLAY, "wake"});
113: #endif
114:     }
115:     else
116:     {
117:         models_ = models_list;
118:         ParseWakenetModelConfig();
119:     }
120: 
121:     if (models_ == nullptr || models_->num == -1)
122:     {
123:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to initialize wakenet model");
124:         return false;
125:     }
126: 
127:     // 初始化 multinet (命令词识别)
128:     mn_name_ = esp_srmodel_filter(models_, ESP_MN_PREFIX, language_.c_str());
129:     if (mn_name_ == nullptr)
130:     {
131:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Language '%s' multinet not found, falling back to any multinet model", language_.c_str());
132:         mn_name_ = esp_srmodel_filter(models_, ESP_MN_PREFIX, NULL);
133:     }
134:     if (mn_name_ == nullptr)
135:     {
136:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to initialize multinet, mn_name is nullptr");
137:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Please refer to https://pcn7cs20v8cr.feishu.cn/wiki/CpQjwQsCJiQSWSkYEvrcxcbVnwh to add custom wake word");
138:         return false;
139:     }
140: 
141:     multinet_ = esp_mn_handle_from_name(mn_name_);
142:     multinet_model_data_ = multinet_->create(mn_name_, duration_);
143:     multinet_->set_det_threshold(multinet_model_data_, threshold_);
144:     esp_mn_commands_clear();
145:     for (int i = 0; i < commands_.size(); i++)
146:     {
147:         esp_mn_commands_add(i + 1, commands_[i].command.c_str());
148:     }
149:     esp_mn_commands_update();
150: 
151:     multinet_->print_active_speech_commands(multinet_model_data_);
152:     return true;
153: }

155: void CustomWakeWord::OnWakeWordDetected(std::function<void(const std::string &wake_word)> callback)
156: {
157:     wake_word_detected_callback_ = callback;
158: }

160: void CustomWakeWord::Start()
161: {
162:     running_ = true;
163: }
164: 
165: void CustomWakeWord::Stop()
166: {
167:     running_ = false;
168: }
169: 
170: void CustomWakeWord::Feed(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
171: {
172:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr || !running_)
173:     {
174:         return;
175:     }
176: 
177:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
178:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
179:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
180:     {
181:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
182:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
183:         {
184:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
185:         }
186: 
187:         StoreWakeWordData(mono_data);
188:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));
189:     }
190:     else
191:     {
192:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
193:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
194:     }
195: 
196:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
197:     {
198:         return;
199:     }
200:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
201:     {
202:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
203:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
204:         {
205:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
206:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
207:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
208:             if (command.action == "wake")
209:             {
210:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
211:                 running_ = false;
212: 
213:                 if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
214:                 {
215:                     wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
216:                 }
217:             }
218:         }
219:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
220:     }
221:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
222:     {
223:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
224:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
225:     }
226: }

228: size_t CustomWakeWord::GetFeedSize()
229: {
230:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr)
231:     {
232:         return 0;
233:     }
234:     return multinet_->get_samp_chunksize(multinet_model_data_);
235: }

237: void CustomWakeWord::StoreWakeWordData(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
238: {
239:     // store audio data to wake_word_pcm_
240:     wake_word_pcm_.push_back(data);
241:     // keep about 2 seconds of data, detect duration is 30ms (sample_rate == 16000, chunksize == 512)
242:     while (wake_word_pcm_.size() > 2000 / 30)
243:     {
244:         wake_word_pcm_.pop_front();
245:     }
246: }
247: if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
248: {
249:     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate encode task stack");
250:     return;
251: }
252: }
253: 
254: if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
255: {
256:     wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = static_cast<char *>(heap_caps_malloc(CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD_ENCODE_TASK_BUFFER_SIZE, MALLOC_CAP_DEFAULT));
257:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
258:     {
259:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate encode task buffer");
260:         return;
261:     }
262: }
263: 
264: // Start the encode task
265: xTaskCreate(EncodeWakeWordDataTask, "encode_wake_word",
266:             CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD_ENCODE_TASK_STACK_SIZE,
267:             this,
268:             CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD_ENCODE_TASK_PRIORITY,
269:             &wake_word_encode_task_handle_);
270: 
271: // Process PCM data and encode to OPUS
272: std::vector<int16_t> pcm_data;
273: size_t total_samples = 0;
274: 
275: // Combine all PCM chunks into one continuous buffer
276: for (const auto &chunk : wake_word_pcm_)
277: {
278:     pcm_data.insert(pcm_data.end(), chunk.begin(), chunk.end());
279:     total_samples += chunk.size();
280: }
281: 
282: if (pcm_data.empty())
283: {
284:     return;
285: }
286: 
287: // Get audio codec parameters
288: int sample_rate = codec_->sample_rate();
289: int channels = codec_->output_channels();
290: 
291: // Encode to OPUS format
292: // Note: This assumes we have access to an OPUS encoder
293: // In practice, this would use the ESP-IDF OPUS encoder or similar
294: 
295: // For now, create a placeholder implementation that would normally do actual encoding
296: // This is a simplified version - in real implementation, you'd use actual OPUS encoding
297: std::vector<uint8_t> opus_data;
298: 
299: // Convert PCM to OPUS (this would be actual encoding logic)
300: // For demonstration, we'll simulate encoding with a simple approach
301: // In reality, you'd use something like:
302: // opus_data = EncodeToOpus(pcm_data.data(), total_samples, sample_rate, channels);
303: 
304: // Add the encoded data to our queue
305: std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
306: wake_word_opus_.push_back(opus_data);
307: wake_word_cv_.notify_one();
308: }
309: bool CustomWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
310: {
311:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
312:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
313:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
314:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
315:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
316:     return !opus.empty();
317: }
318:

The generated code is virtually identical to the ground truth code with only minor stylistic differences that don't affect functionality.

Semantic Correctness (100/100): Both codes implement the exact same logic - acquiring a mutex lock, waiting on a condition variable until the wake word queue is not empty, swapping the front element into the output parameter, removing the front element, and returning whether the resulting vector is non-empty. The semantic meaning and logical flow are completely identical.

Structure Similarity (95/100): The structure is nearly identical with only cosmetic differences in formatting. The function signature, mutex locking pattern, condition variable usage, container operations, and return statement are all exactly the same. The only difference is in whitespace formatting around the lambda expression and the closing brace placement, which doesn't impact structural integrity.

Functional Completeness (100/100): All key functionalities are preserved including proper synchronization with mutex and condition variable, correct use of std::vector::swap for efficient transfer, proper queue management with pop_front(), and accurate return value determination based on whether the opus vector is empty after the swap operation.

46:         else

Semantic Correctness (95/100): The generated code correctly implements the core logic of setting output states. Both codes follow the same fundamental approach - determining which register (0x02 or 0x03) to access based on the bit number, reading the current register value, modifying the specific bit, and writing back. The bit manipulation logic is functionally equivalent, though the generated code uses modulo arithmetic (bit % 8) instead of explicit index calculation, which is slightly less efficient but still correct. The only minor difference is that the ground truth calculates index = bit and then subtracts 8 for bits >= 8, while the generated code directly uses bit % 8.

Structure Similarity (85/100): The structural similarity is high but not perfect. Both functions have identical signatures and basic control flow patterns. However, there are notable differences: the ground truth uses a uint16_t data variable while the generated uses uint8_t, the ground truth calculates index explicitly while the generated uses bit % 8 directly, and the ground truth has more explicit conditional checks. The naming conventions differ slightly (index vs port), and the generated code has a more concise bit manipulation approach using |= and &= operators instead of the more verbose bitwise operations.

Functional Completeness (90/100): The generated code covers all essential functionalities from the ground truth. Both handle the two-port addressing scheme correctly, read from the appropriate registers, perform bit manipulation, and write back to the correct registers. The functionality is complete, though the generated code's use of bit % 8 instead of explicit index calculation makes it slightly less readable and potentially more error-prone if bit values exceed the expected range. The generated code also handles edge cases appropriately, including proper bit masking and setting/clearing logic.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85/100): The generated code maintains the same core functionality as the ground truth - initializing an SPI bus for LCD display communication. Both set up the same pin configurations (MOSI, SCLK) and call the same initialization function. However, there are some semantic differences: the generated code uses -1 instead of GPIO_NUM_NC for unused pins, and sets max_transfer_sz to 0 instead of calculating it based on display dimensions. The flags field is also added in the generated version which isn't in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity (75/100): The structural similarity is moderate. Both functions have the same signature and follow the same logical flow. However, there are notable differences: the ground truth uses a simpler initialization syntax with braces, while the generated code uses designated initializers with explicit field names. The generated code also includes additional fields like flags and intr_flags that aren't present in the ground truth. Formatting and comment styles differ significantly.

Functional Completeness (70/100): The generated code covers most key functionalities but misses critical elements. The most significant omission is the calculation of max_transfer_sz based on DISPLAY_WIDTH * DISPLAY_HEIGHT * sizeof(uint16_t), which is crucial for proper SPI transfer sizing. The generated code hardcodes this value to 0. Additionally, the generated code introduces extra configuration parameters (flags, intr_flags) not present in the ground truth, though these don't break functionality. The use of -1 instead of GPIO_NUM_NC for unused pins is a minor functional difference.

- The generated code does not implement the exact hardware control sequence described in the ground truth.

28: // FrequencyDetector implementation
29: FrequencyDetector::FrequencyDetector(float frequency, size_t window_size)
30:     : frequency_(frequency), window_size_(window_size)
31: {
32:     frequency_bin_ = std::floor(frequency_ * static_cast<float>(window_size_));
33:     angular_frequency_ = 2.0f * M_PI * frequency_;
34:     cos_coefficient_ = std::cos(angular_frequency_);
35:     sin_coefficient_ = std::sin(angular_frequency_);
36:     filter_coefficient_ = 2.0f * cos_coefficient_;
37: 
38:     // Initialize state buffer
39:     state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
40:     state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
41: }
42: 
43: void FrequencyDetector::Reset()
44: {
45:     state_buffer_.clear();
46:     state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
47:     state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
48: }
49: 
50: void FrequencyDetector::ProcessSample(float sample)
51: {
52:     if (state_buffer_.size() < 2)
53:     {
54:         return;
55:     }
56: 
57:     float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-2]
58:     state_buffer_.pop_front();
59:     float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-1]
60:     state_buffer_.pop_front();
61: 
62:     float s_current = sample + filter_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2;
63: 
64:     state_buffer_.push_back(s_minus_1); // Put S[-1] back
65:     state_buffer_.push_back(s_current); // Add new S[0]
66: }
67: 
68: float FrequencyDetector::GetAmplitude() const
69: {
70:     if (state_buffer_.size() < 2)
71:     {
72:         return 0.0f;
73:     }
74: 
75:     float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_[1];                         // S[-1]
76:     float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_[0];                         // S[-2]
77:     float real_part = cos_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2; // Real part
78:     float imaginary_part = sin_coefficient_ * s_minus_1;        // Imaginary part
79: 
80:     return std::sqrt(real_part * real_part + imaginary_part * imaginary_part) /
81:            (static_cast<float>(window_size_) / 2.0f);
82: }

194:     {
195:         uint8_t bit = (probability > threshold) ? 1 : 0;
196: 
197:         if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= identifier_buffer_size_)
198:         {

200:         }
201:         identifier_buffer_.push_back(bit);
202: 
203:         // Process received bit based on state machine
204:         switch (current_state_)
205:         {
206:         case DataReceptionState::kInactive:
207:             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
208:             {
209:                 current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kWaiting; // Enter waiting state
210:                 ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Waiting state");
211:             }
212:             break;
213: 
214:         case DataReceptionState::kWaiting:
215:             // Waiting state, possibly waiting for transmission end
216:             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
217:             {
218:                 std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
219:                 if (identifier_snapshot == start_of_transmission_)
220:                 {
221:                     ClearBuffers();                                  // Clear buffers
222:                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kReceiving; // Enter receiving state
223:                     ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Receiving state");
224:                 }
225:             }
226:             break;
227: 
228:         case DataReceptionState::kReceiving:
229:             bit_buffer_.push_back(bit);
230:             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= end_of_transmission_.size())
231:             {
232:                 std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
233:                 if (identifier_snapshot == end_of_transmission_)
234:                 {
235:                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Enter inactive state
236: 
237:                     // Convert bits to bytes
238:                     std::vector<uint8_t> bytes = ConvertBitsToBytes(bit_buffer_);
239: 
240:                     uint8_t received_checksum = 0;
241:                     size_t minimum_length = 0;
242: 
243:                     if (enable_checksum_validation_)
244:                     {
245:                         // If checksum is required, last byte is checksum
246:                         minimum_length = 1 + start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
247:                         if (bytes.size() >= minimum_length)
248:                         {
249:                             received_checksum = bytes[bytes.size() - start_of_transmission_.size() / 8 - 1];
250:                         }
251:                     }
252:                     else
253:                     {
254:                         minimum_length = start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
255:                     }
256: 
257:                     if (bytes.size() < minimum_length)
258:                     {
259:                         ClearBuffers();
260:                         ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Data too short, clearing buffer");
261:                         return false; // Data too short, return failure
262:                     }
263: 
264:                     // Extract text data (remove trailing identifier part)
265:                     std::vector<uint8_t> text_bytes(
266:                         bytes.begin(), bytes.begin() + bytes.size() - minimum_length);
267: 
268:                     std::string result(text_bytes.begin(), text_bytes.end());
269: 
270:                     // Validate checksum if required
271:                     if (enable_checksum_validation_)
272:                     {
273:                         uint8_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(result);
274:                         if (calculated_checksum != received_checksum)
275:                         {
276:                             // Checksum mismatch
277:                             ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Checksum mismatch: expected %d, got %d",
278:                                      received_checksum, calculated_checksum);
279:                             ClearBuffers();
280:                             return false;
281:                         }
282:                     }
283: 
284:                     ClearBuffers();
285:                     decoded_text = result;
286:                     return true; // Return success
287:                 }
288:                 else if (bit_buffer_.size() >= max_bit_buffer_size_)
289:                 {
290:                     // If not end identifier and bit buffer is full, reset
291:                     ClearBuffers();
292:                     ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Buffer overflow, clearing buffer");
293:                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Reset state machine
294:                 }
295:             }
296:             break;
297:         }
298:     }
299: 
300:     return false;
301: }
302: 
303: std::vector<uint8_t> AudioDataBuffer::ConvertBitsToBytes(const std::vector<uint8_t> &bits) const
304: {
305:     std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
306: 
307:     // Ensure number of bits is a multiple of 8
308:     size_t complete_bytes_count = bits.size() / 8;
309:     bytes.reserve(complete_bytes_count);
310: 
311:     for (size_t i = 0; i < complete_bytes_count; ++i)
312:     {
313:         uint8_t byte_value = 0;
314:         for (size_t j = 0; j < 8; ++j)
315:         {
316:             byte_value |= bits[i * 8 + j] << (7 - j);
317:         }
318:         bytes.push_back(byte_value);
319:     }
320: 
321:     return bytes;
322: }
323: }

It also doesn't handle the configuration mode exit or use the proper logging mechanisms. The code is essentially a skeleton that only implements basic UI updates but misses the entire audio processing pipeline.
